title: Accumulating
checkLabels.t5: The number is not typed in
checkLabels.t6: The cups use the short form
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: The sales are 12, 8, 15 and 5. Add them up in your head and look at what the console shows.
why.not-closed: A tag in index.html is open and never closed.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
why.typed-answer: You typed the total in instead of accumulating it.
hint2.typed-answer: Tomorrow the sales will be different. The lines have to add up, not announce the result.
why.long-cups: The cups are still added the long way.
hint2.long-cups: cups = cups + 12 can be written shorter. The plus sign goes in front of the equals sign.
why.long-stock: The stock is still calculated the long way.
why.long-orders: The orders are still counted the long way.
hint2.long-orders: Adding one has a sign of its own. Two pluses stuck together.
hint2.wrong-cups: 12 plus 8 plus 15 plus 5 makes 40. Read the four lines one at a time and find the one that does not add.
hint3.wrong-cups: One of the lines does not accumulate, it replaces. Everything gathered before it disappears and the counting starts over.
hint4.wrong-cups: Look very closely at the line with 15. The two signs are the wrong way round.
hint2.wrong-stock: The stock starts at 50 and the sales are taken off it. If the sales are wrong, the remainder is wrong too.
why.wrong-count: There are not three lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A line has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: Four sales are added into a total. Then the sales are taken off the stock, and the orders are counted one at a time.
blocks.1.text: The code runs. The console shows three numbers and not one of them is right. Add the four sales up in your head and compare.
blocks.3.text: Take the value, add something, put it back under the same name. This happens so often in code that it has its own form.
blocks.5.text: Both lines do the same thing. The second is not read left to right like a sentence — it is read as an action: add twelve to the cups.
blocks.7.text: Adding one is even more common. It has a sign of its own, and you will need it in every loop from section four onwards.
blocks.9.text: One up, one down. Nothing more.
blocks.11.text: The plus sign goes BEFORE the equals sign. The other way round is still valid code — but it means something completely different.
blocks.12.text: Reversed, they do not add. They replace. Everything gathered so far disappears and nobody complains, because the line is perfectly correct — it just does not do what you thought.
blocks.13.text: Two signs, swapped around. That is the difference between a correct total and a silent loss.
blocks.15.text: Rewrite everything with the short form: the adding, the subtracting and the counting. You will have to read each line separately.
blocks.16.text: That is the point. One of them hides the bug, and you will only see it if you read slowly.
